dimensions.description: How many files/components must be modified or created?

analysis.reasoning: Moderate scope. Core payment functionality integrated into appointment & patient modules. Clear separation of concerns (API routes, components, utilities).

dimensions.description: How many external APIs must be integrated? Authentication? Webhook handling?

external_apis.name: Stripe API
external_apis.why: PaymentIntent creation, SetupIntent for tokenization, webhook signature validation, error handling, idempotency keys required

external_apis.name: Email Service (Resend/SendGrid)
external_apis.why: Simple send() call. Already in use in the project (Resend confirmed). Invoice as PDF attachment is the only novel part.

external_apis.name: Supabase PostgreSQL
external_apis.why: New tables (invoices, payment_methods) but standard schema. RLS policies required but straightforward.

webhook_requirements: Must validate webhook signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Must handle payment_intent.succeeded and payment_intent.payment_failed events
Must implement idempotency (prevent double-processing same event)
Must handle timeouts gracefully (Stripe expects 30s response)
analysis.reasoning: HIGH complexity. Stripe webhook reliability is critical. Idempotency and signature validation are non-trivial. Email integration is simple, but PDF generation adds complexity.

dimensions.description: Must database schema be altered? Deployment changes? Environment variables?

tables_to_add: invoices (id, appointment_id, clinic_id, patient_id, invoice_number, amount_cents, currency, pdf_url, sent_at, created_at)
payment_methods (id, patient_id, clinic_id, stripe_payment_method_id, card_last_4, brand, exp_month, exp_year, is_default, created_at)
payment_audit_log (id, clinic_id, user_id, operation, payment_method_id, amount_cents, success, result, created_at)

deployment_changes: Webhook endpoint must be publicly accessible at fixed URL
Environment variables must be set in production
PDF generation may require additional dependencies (puppeteer, html2pdf) — check bundle size
analysis.reasoning: MODERATE complexity. Database changes are standard (3 new tables, 1 modified). RLS policies straightforward. Webhook endpoint requires no new infrastructure. Key risk: PDF library bundle size.

dimensions.description: How familiar is the team with Stripe API, PCI compliance, PDF generation?

team_experience.stripe_api: LOW - No current Stripe integration in the codebase. Team has not implemented PaymentIntent or SetupIntent before. Stripe webhook handling is new.
team_experience.pci_compliance: LOW - No existing PCI audit in the project. Team unfamiliar with PCI DSS requirements. External auditor may be required.
team_experience.pdf_generation: LOW - html2pdf/Puppeteer not yet in the project. Image rendering (logo, charts) in PDF is a known pain point.
team_experience.webhook_handling: MEDIUM - Existing webhook handling for WhatsApp in EPIC-005. Similar pattern can be reused (signature validation, idempotency).
team_experience.typescript_react: HIGH - Team is proficient in Next.js, TypeScript, React.

learning_curve: PDF generation library selection and testing (3-5 hours)
analysis.reasoning: HIGH learning curve. Stripe API and PCI compliance are new to the team. However, team has strong TypeScript/React foundation. External guidance (Stripe docs, PCI auditor) readily available.

dimensions.description: Payment processing is business-critical. Failure = no revenue. Risk of PCI non-compliance = legal liability.

analysis.business_criticality: CRITICAL - Payments are revenue stream. Failure blocks clinic operations.
compliance_risk: PCI DSS non-compliance: Legal penalties + card network fines ($1000-100K per incident)
Data breach risk: Card data exposure would be catastrophic (LGPD + PCI)
Webhook reliability: Failed payments must be detected and retried. Loss of webhook = lost revenue.
technical_risks: Idempotency bug: Duplicate charges if webhook retry not handled → customer refunds + reputation damage
Stripe error handling: Declined cards, timeouts, network failures must not lose payment state
Invoice generation: If PDF fails, customer has no receipt → support tickets
Integration with appointment workflow: Payment status must sync correctly with appointment state
mitigation_strategy: Stripe-provided testing environment (test cards, test webhooks)
Pre-launch security audit (PCI DSS self-assessment + external auditor)
Comprehensive error scenarios testing (declined card, timeout, network failure, duplicate webhook)
Audit logging for all payment operations (immutable)
Staged rollout: Internal testing → UAT with clinic staff → Go-live
analysis.reasoning: CRITICAL risk. Payment processing is highest-risk feature. PCI compliance is non-negotiable. Strong testing and audit strategy required.

complexity_calculation.class_definition: COMPLEX (≥16): High scope, multiple external integrations, significant knowledge gaps, critical business risk. Requires 6-phase spec pipeline with detailed architecture & risk review.

rationale: Integration with Stripe API is HIGH complexity (webhook reliability, idempotency, error handling)
PCI DSS compliance is non-negotiable and team has low familiarity
Payment processing is CRITICAL to business — failure = no revenue
Multiple external dependencies (Stripe, Email service, PDF generation)
Scope: 15 files affected across API, components, database, migrations
classification_details.phases_required: All 6 phases (gather → assess → research → write → critique → plan)

next_steps: ASSIGN to @analyst: Stripe API v2024-04 deep-dive, PCI DSS requirements, webhook best practices, PDF generation options
TIMELINE: Phase 3 research should complete by 2026-05-18 EOD
PHASE 4 GATE: Write spec.md only after Phase 3 research is complete
